title: Merck — Projects
html:
Module 5: Active Projects & Workstream Intelligence

Governing Thought

The Merck account has 10+ active or recently active project workstreams spanning oncology (Keytruda), HIV, RSV (Enflonsia), transplant (Prevymis), and vaccines (Gardasil/VAXELIS). Unlike J&J where ZoomRx runs 14+ concurrent workstreams with deep multi-year baselines, the Merck portfolio is characterized by franchise concentration (Keytruda dominates volume), recent new-franchise entries (HIV global RT, Enflonsia PET, Prevymis KT), and significant upcoming launch-driven work (IDVYNSO SFE in 2H26, KN-689 continuation, QLEX adoption tracking). Three themes cut across the portfolio: (1) Merck's launches create natural tracking demand that ZoomRx is well-positioned to capture; (2) the cross-study insights from Keytruda oncology studies (referral chain bottleneck, PD-L1 testing maturity, MDT dynamics) are ZoomRx's most defensible intellectual property on this account; and (3) franchise diversification beyond Keytruda is essential to protect against the LOE cliff (2028-2029). Vinoth's role is ensuring East 7 delivers flawlessly on active projects while supporting Dave/Melissa on expansion pitches.

Account Health Dashboard

Workstream Status Last Deliverable Key Finding
Keytruda NSCLC PET :green_circle: Active [Populate wave date] Core recurring tracker — must renew
KN-689 HNSCC HCP-Pt + Qual :green_circle: Active Feb 2026 (Final Report v4) Surgeon referral bottleneck confirmed; PD-L1 testing 4/13 reflexive
KN-689 Listening Sessions :green_circle: Active Apr 2026 (ongoing waves) Longitudinal HCP-Pt conversation collection
Keytruda NSCLC HCP-Pt Study :white_circle: Complete Feb 2025 (vF) Only 59% of Stage 2+ referred; 53% no MDT mention
LA HNSCC Biomarker Testing Qual :white_circle: Complete Jan 2025 (v7.0) Only 3/35 reflexive PD-L1; FNA biopsy a barrier
HNSCC ATU Wave 1 (Baseline) :white_circle: Complete Mar 2025 (n=134) 82% ONC self-order biomarkers; 50% no unaided awareness
SubQ IO HCP-Pt Conversations :yellow_circle: Early Nov 2025 (kickoff) 7 conversations collected — too early for patterns
Keytruda Message Testing V2 :blue_circle: Pipeline Apr 2026 (proposal) Global + US; in proposal stage
HIV Global RT (DOR+ISL) :green_circle: Active Mar 2026 ($125K won) First global win at Merck
HIV SFE (DOR/ISL) :blue_circle: Planned — 2 waves, 2H 2026, N=90/wave; Wave 1 topline July 27
Enflonsia PET :green_circle: Active Q1 2026 (Wave 2) Beyfortus reach 80% vs Enflonsia 50%; dosing message strongest
Enflonsia Digital Tracking :green_circle: Active Q1 2026 RSV = 2% of browsing; Enflonsia gaining paid search share
Prevymis KT ATU :green_circle: Active 2024 (Wave 2) KT expansion driving growth; myelosuppression message anchor
Prevymis KT PET/Message Recall :green_circle: Active May-Aug 2025 (Wave 1) Prevymis leads SOV; KT7 most effective but under-recalled
Prevymis KT Qual (Patient) :green_circle: Active [Populate date] Patient perspective on KT CMV prophylaxis
Lynparza mCRPC PET :green_circle: Active 2026 [Populate key finding]
Gardasil PET :yellow_circle: Revisiting Historical (2021-2024 VAXELIS) Revisiting PET — could reactivate

Legend: :green_circle: Active/On Track | :yellow_circle: Watch/Early | :red_circle: Risk | :blue_circle: Pipeline/Planned | :white_circle: Complete

Detailed Workstream Profiles

Oncology — Keytruda

Keytruda NSCLC PET

Status: Active (recurring) | Latest: [Populate most recent wave] | Brand: Keytruda

What it is: Promotional Effectiveness Tracking for Keytruda in NSCLC — the core recurring engagement on the account.

What's working:

  • Established tracker with multi-wave baseline data
  • NSCLC is Keytruda's largest revenue driver (~40% of $31.7B franchise) — tracking is essential to client

Watch items:

  • Must-renew in FY26 — losing this would be a material revenue event
  • Opportunity to expand into HNSCC, melanoma, or other tumor types as line extensions to PET

Recs:

  • Brief Dave/Melissa on tumor type expansion angles (HNSCC, melanoma) for next Rhonda Harp conversation
  • Prepare pricing for multi-tumor PET bundle

SharePoint: Project #29

KN-689 HNSCC — HCP-Pt Conversations + Qualitative + ATU

Status: Active (multi-study workstream) | Latest: Final Report v4 (Feb 2026) + ATU W1 (Mar 2025) | Brand: Keytruda (HNSCC indication)

What it is: The most comprehensive HNSCC insight package in the industry — combining HCP-Patient conversations (n=7), qualitative TDIs (n=14), quantitative ATU (n=134), biomarker testing qual (n=35), and ongoing listening sessions. This is ZoomRx's intellectual crown jewel on the Merck account.

What's working:

  • Cross-study synthesis identified 6 distinct patterns: (1) referral chain as universal bottleneck, (2) PD-L1 testing maturity predicts adoption, (3) surgeon-oncologist parallel universes, (4) KN-689 drivers are both immediate and future-oriented, (5) community vs. academic as defining adoption axis, (6) unaided awareness is surgeon-led but ~50% recall nothing
  • ATU W1 baseline provides quantitative foundation: 82% ONCs self-order biomarkers, 85% SURGs present to MDT vs 65% ONC perception, 70% have KN-689 knowledge
  • Post-launch HCP experience is positive: improved margins, reduced burden, favorable tolerability

Watch items:

  • ATU W2 is overdue — fielded Aug 2025 but post-launch quantitative adoption data still pending as of Apr 2026. This is the most important pending deliverable on the account.
  • SubQ IO study has only 7 conversations — too small for pattern detection. QLEX J-code (Apr 2026) should accelerate.
  • No competitor IO tracking in HNSCC — Opdivo awareness at 61-63% but not tracked against prescribing intent
  • No longitudinal surgeon behavior tracking — the most important gap identified across all studies

Recs:

  • Push for ATU W2 release if completed; if not fielded, escalate
  • Pitch surgeon-specific study to Merck (fills the #1 identified gap)
  • Propose cross-indication perioperative tracking platform (NSCLC + HNSCC + urothelial)

SharePoint: Projects #37 (HNSCC HCP-Pt), plus multiple files

Keytruda Message Testing V2

Status: Pipeline (Apr 2026 proposal) | Brand: Keytruda

What it is: Global + US message testing and optimization — builds on existing messaging work.

Watch items:

  • In proposal stage — track decision timeline closely
  • This could be $100-150K of new revenue

SubQ IO HCP-Pt Conversations

Status: Active (early) | Latest: Nov 2025 (kickoff) | Brand: Keytruda (pan-tumor)

What it is: Tracking real HCP-Patient conversations about QLEX (subcutaneous Keytruda) across NSCLC, melanoma, TNBC, RCC, urothelial.

What's working:

  • Foot in the door for QLEX adoption tracking — strategically important for Merck's LOE defense
  • Permanent J-code (J9277) effective April 1, 2026 should accelerate conversations

Watch items:

  • Only 7 conversations collected (4 MD, 3 NP/PA) — too early for patterns
  • QLEX Q4 2025 sales only $35M out of $8.4B total Keytruda (<0.5% penetration) — adoption is slow
  • Key question: Is SubQ being positioned as "alternate" or default?

Recs:

  • Post-J-code pulse check in Q2-Q3 2026 — this is the natural follow-up study
  • If QLEX adoption accelerates, pitch a dedicated QLEX adoption tracker to Merck's LOE defense team

SharePoint: Project #39

Lynparza mCRPC PET

Status: Active | Brand: Lynparza (olaparib) — Merck/AstraZeneca

What it is: PET tracking for Lynparza in metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer.

Watch items:

  • Single project — limited growth potential
  • Lynparza is a co-commercialized asset (Merck + AstraZeneca) — budget dynamics may differ

SharePoint: Project #40

HIV

HIV Global RT (DOR+ISL)

Status: Active | Latest: Mar 2026 (won) | Value: $125K | Brand: IDVYNSO/Pifeltro

What it is: ZoomRx's first global win at Merck — a retainer-style global HCP research tracker for the doravirine/islatravir franchise.

What's working:

  • Template for future global work at Merck
  • Retainer model = sticky, recurring revenue
  • Positions ZoomRx as the IDVYNSO launch tracking partner

Watch items:

  • Renewal is high-priority — this engagement must be flawless to anchor the HIV relationship
  • Opportunity to add additional markets or waves

SharePoint: Project #41

HIV SFE Tracking (DOR/ISL — Planned)

Status: Planned — not yet fielded | Design: 2 waves, 2H 2026, N=90 unique HCPs/wave | Brand: IDVYNSO

What it is: Promotional Effectiveness Tracking for IDVYNSO post-launch (May 2026). Tracks DOR/ISL vs Dovato vs Biktarvy — the first external measurement of IDVYNSO's commercial execution.

Key design decisions:

  • IDVYNSO expected approved late April 2026, promotions beginning May 1
  • 4-6 week buffer before fielding
  • Wave 1 topline: week of July 27, 2026
  • Wave 1 full report: week of September 7, 2026
  • Wave 2 topline: week of November 23, 2026
  • Wave 2 full report: week of January 11, 2027

Critical questions for Wave 1:

  1. Which IDVYNSO messages are being delivered? (niche: lipid/renal vs. platform: non-inferior to Biktarvy)
  2. Has Merck's reach improved from Pifeltro's ~65%?
  3. What is IDVYNSO sampling penetration vs. Biktarvy's 90%?
  4. Is islatravir perception risk suppressing prescribing intent?
  5. NP/PA reach — has Merck expanded into this segment?

Recs from HIV synthesis:

  • Recommend Merck track message framing ratio ("non-inferior to Biktarvy" vs "lipid/renal")
  • Push for reach/frequency deep-dive in Wave 1 report
  • Recommend including islatravir safety confidence question
  • Recommend NP/PA oversample

HIV Pifeltro MR Studies (Historical)

Status: Complete / transitioning to IDVYNSO | Brand: Pifeltro

Key findings from final studies (Aug-Dec 2025):

  • Merck SOV: 28% (vs Gilead 42%, ViiV 30%) — structural disadvantage
  • Merck reach: ~65% monthly (vs Gilead ~80%) — 15-point gap
  • Rep performance improved significantly: +16% call quality, +16% answering questions, +14% preparedness
  • Rep closing: 52% (on par with Dovato, above Biktarvy 41%)
  • Pifeltro message effectiveness ceiling: 53% avg ME vs Dovato 67% and Biktarvy 75%
  • Patient type targeting (weight, comorbidities, lipids, renal) is Pifeltro's strongest differentiation

Legacy insight: These Pifeltro studies provide the baseline against which IDVYNSO Wave 1 will be measured. The central question: can Merck's field force pivot from niche (comorbidity/tolerability) to platform (head-to-head Biktarvy efficacy)?

RSV / Vaccines

Enflonsia PET

Status: Active | Latest: Q1 2026 (Wave 2) | Brand: Enflonsia (clesrovimab)

What it is: Promotional Effectiveness Tracking for Enflonsia vs Beyfortus in infant RSV prevention.

What's working:

  • Enflonsia's dosing message (ENF2: same dose regardless of weight) is highest-recall and highest-effectiveness — 79% ME, 84% recall
  • Enflonsia SoV growing directionally Q4 to Q1
  • Enflonsia reps close >90% of interactions

Watch items:

  • Beyfortus reach significantly higher: 80% PP reach vs. Enflonsia 50% — 30-point gap
  • Beyfortus NPP gap even wider: 95% reach vs 41% Enflonsia
  • Only ~33% of HCPs report high likelihood to stock Enflonsia
  • Enflonsia reps trail Beyfortus on knowledge, credibility, question-handling
  • Efficacy perception favors Beyfortus (74.9% vs 60.5% MA-LRTI reduction — not head-to-head but directionally clear)
  • CDC schedule change: 2/3 of HCPs expect number receiving RSV prevention will decline

Recs:

  • Feed back rep performance gaps to Merck for training investment
  • Pre-season window (July-September) is when stocking decisions are made — ensure Wave 3 timing captures this
  • Push for RWE integration once available — Enflonsia has zero real-world evidence vs Beyfortus's 40+ studies

SharePoint: Project #36

Enflonsia Digital Tracking

Status: Active | Latest: Q1 2026 | Brand: Enflonsia

What it is: Passive tracking of HCP digital interactions — browsing, paid search, emails, banner ads.

Key findings:

  • RSV browsing = ~2% of total healthcare browsing among target HCPs
  • Beyfortus dominates brand-related browsing time
  • Enflonsia gaining paid search share: 22% on general RSV terms, 27% on Beyfortus keyword terms
  • Banner ad exposure is sparse: 94% of HCPs saw no market-basket RSV ads

SharePoint: Project #38

Gardasil PET

Status: Revisiting | Latest: Historical (VAXELIS PET ran 2021-2024) | Brand: Gardasil 9

What it is: Potential reactivation of HPV vaccine PET after a period of inactivity.

Context: Gardasil FY2025 revenue dropped 39% to $5.2B (China collapse). The PET revisit may be defensive — maintaining franchise visibility during a downturn. If Merck is re-investing in Gardasil post-competitive pressure, this could close fast.

Estimated value: $80-120K Action: Get status from Melissa on Gardasil PET reactivation timeline and decision-maker.

Transplant

Prevymis KT ATU + PET/Message Recall + Patient Qual

Status: Active (multiple studies) | Latest: PET Wave 1 (May-Aug 2025), ATU Wave 2 (2024) | Brand: Prevymis (letermovir)

What it is: A multi-study workstream tracking Prevymis adoption in kidney transplant CMV prophylaxis — ATU, message recall, patient qual.

What's working:

  • Prevymis leads market SOV in both rep interactions and NPP

  • 90% HCP exposure to Prevymis NPP (>40% SOV)

  • KT messages exceed industry averages (68% top message vs. 64% industry avg)
  • 88% of KT interactions drove at least one action
  • Revenue approaching $1B (FY25: $978M, +25%) — franchise is growing fast

Watch items:

  • KT7 is the biggest messaging opportunity: "No CMV cases with Prevymis vs. 5 cases with valganciclovir through Week 28" — highest perceived effectiveness but LOWEST recall. Under-delivery of the most compelling message.
  • HSCT efficacy messages (survival benefit, 200-day efficacy) are also under-recalled relative to effectiveness
  • Cost remains a real barrier: 15-30x premium vs generic valganciclovir
  • Access concerns persist: "One of the major hurdles to obtaining letermovir for my patients is the cost"

Recs:

  • Feed back KT7 under-recall finding explicitly — this is a trainable gap for the Merck field force
  • Push for HSCT efficacy message delivery improvement (HSCT4, HSCT5)
  • Propose cost/access message module or PSP awareness tracking

SharePoint: Project #26

Cross-Workstream Themes

1. Launch Tracking Demand Creates Natural Growth

Three simultaneous Merck launches — IDVYNSO (HIV, May 2026), KN-689 (HNSCC, ongoing post-June 2025), and Enflonsia (RSV, launched July 2025) — all require tracking research. ZoomRx is already positioned on two of three (KN-689 and Enflonsia) and has the SFE study locked for IDVYNSO. The launch wave is the most favorable BD environment for the account.

Implication for Vinoth: Ensure East 7 capacity can absorb the workload from these three concurrent launches. If a new win comes in (Gardasil PET reactivation), staffing becomes the constraint.

2. Keytruda Cross-Study Insights Are the Defensible Moat

The cross-study synthesis across 9 Keytruda studies represents ZoomRx's most defensible intellectual property on the account. No competitor can replicate the referral chain analysis, PD-L1 testing trajectory, MDT dynamics mapping, or community vs. academic adoption patterns without years of longitudinal access.

Implication for Vinoth: This cross-study capability should be surfaced to Rhonda Harp as evidence of ZoomRx's strategic value. It is the strongest argument for elevating ZoomRx from "tracking vendor" to "insights partner."

3. Under-Delivered High-Impact Messages Are a Common Pattern

Both Prevymis (KT7: "No CMV cases") and Enflonsia (ENF2: dosing simplicity — well-delivered but ENF10 under-recalled) show a pattern where the most effective messages are not the most frequently delivered. In HIV, Biktarvy's most effective messages are also under-recalled, but Gilead still leads on perceived effectiveness — structural positioning advantage.

Implication for Vinoth: Frame this as a cross-franchise insight in conversations with Merck: "We see a consistent pattern across your brands where your most compelling messages are under-delivered. This is trainable — and we can help you prioritize."

4. Franchise Diversification Is Essential for LOE Protection

Keytruda dominates the Merck account revenue. With LOE in 2028-2029, ZoomRx's Merck revenue is structurally exposed to Keytruda's franchise health. Diversifying into HIV (IDVYNSO), vaccines (Enflonsia, Gardasil), transplant (Prevymis), and pipeline (EyeBio, PCSK-9) is not just a growth strategy — it's a risk management imperative.

Implication for Vinoth: Track non-Keytruda revenue as a % of total Merck revenue. Target >40% non-Keytruda by FY28.

Immediate Priorities

  1. Ensure flawless execution on all active Merck projects. East 7 delivery quality is Vinoth's most important BD contribution. Any delivery stumble during cost-cutting is existential for the account.

  2. Track the IDVYNSO SFE study setup. The SFE study design is locked; fielding begins 4-6 weeks post-approval (late May/early June). Wave 1 topline due week of July 27. Ensure the team is ready.

  3. Push for KN-689 ATU W2 status. Post-launch quantitative data is overdue. If fielded, get the release timeline. If not fielded, escalate — this is the single most valuable pending deliverable.

  4. Prepare Keytruda expansion one-pager. Dave/Melissa need a concise document on adding HNSCC or melanoma to PET. Make it easy for Melissa to pitch in her next Rhonda Harp conversation.

  5. Monitor Gardasil PET reactivation. If genuinely being revisited, this is $80-120K of fast revenue. Get status from Melissa.

  6. Check East 7 capacity. With 6+ active Merck projects and IDVYNSO SFE starting, does East 7 have bandwidth? If not, Vinoth needs a staffing plan before the work arrives.

Back to Accounts Hub | Home

RSV / Vaccines

Enflonsia PET
Status: Active | Latest: Q1 2026 (Wave 2) | Brand: Enflonsia (clesrovimab)
What it is: Promotional Effectiveness Tracking for Enflonsia vs Beyfortus in infant RSV prevention.
What's working:

Enflonsia's dosing message (ENF2: same dose regardless of weight) is highest-recall and highest-effectiveness — 79% ME, 84% recall
Enflonsia SoV growing directionally Q4 to Q1
Enflonsia reps close >90% of interactions

Watch items:

Beyfortus reach significantly higher: 80% PP reach vs. Enflonsia 50% — 30-point gap
Beyfortus NPP gap even wider: 95% reach vs 41% Enflonsia
Only ~33% of HCPs report high likelihood to stock Enflonsia
Enflonsia reps trail Beyfortus on knowledge, credibility, question-handling
Efficacy perception favors Beyfortus (74.9% vs 60.5% MA-LRTI reduction — not head-to-head but directionally clear)
CDC schedule change: 2/3 of HCPs expect number receiving RSV prevention will decline

Recs:

Feed back rep performance gaps to Merck for training investment
Pre-season window (July-September) is when stocking decisions are made — ensure Wave 3 timing captures this
Push for RWE integration once available — Enflonsia has zero real-world evidence vs Beyfortus's 40+ studies

SharePoint: Project #36

Enflonsia Digital Tracking
Status: Active | Latest: Q1 2026 | Brand: Enflonsia
What it is: Passive tracking of HCP digital interactions — browsing, paid search, emails, banner ads.
Key findings:

RSV browsing = ~2% of total healthcare browsing among target HCPs
Beyfortus dominates brand-related browsing time
Enflonsia gaining paid search share: 22% on general RSV terms, 27% on Beyfortus keyword terms
Banner ad exposure is sparse: 94% of HCPs saw no market-basket RSV ads

Gardasil PET

What it is: Potential reactivation of HPV vaccine PET after a period of inactivity.
Context: Gardasil FY2025 revenue dropped 39% to $5.2B (China collapse). The PET revisit may be defensive — maintaining franchise visibility during a downturn. If Merck is re-investing in Gardasil post-competitive pressure, this could close fast.
Estimated value: $80-120K
Action: Get status from Melissa on Gardasil PET reactivation timeline and decision-maker.

Transplant

Prevymis KT ATU + PET/Message Recall + Patient Qual
Status: Active (multiple studies) | Latest: PET Wave 1 (May-Aug 2025), ATU Wave 2 (2024) | Brand: Prevymis (letermovir)
What it is: A multi-study workstream tracking Prevymis adoption in kidney transplant CMV prophylaxis — ATU, message recall, patient qual.
What's working:

Prevymis leads market SOV in both rep interactions and NPP


90% HCP exposure to Prevymis NPP (>40% SOV)


KT messages exceed industry averages (68% top message vs. 64% industry avg)
88% of KT interactions drove at least one action
Revenue approaching $1B (FY25: $978M, +25%) — franchise is growing fast

Watch items:

KT7 is the biggest messaging opportunity: "No CMV cases with Prevymis vs. 5 cases with valganciclovir through Week 28" — highest perceived effectiveness but LOWEST recall. Under-delivery of the most compelling message.
HSCT efficacy messages (survival benefit, 200-day efficacy) are also under-recalled relative to effectiveness
Cost remains a real barrier: 15-30x premium vs generic valganciclovir
Access concerns persist: "One of the major hurdles to obtaining letermovir for my patients is the cost"

Recs:

Feed back KT7 under-recall finding explicitly — this is a trainable gap for the Merck field force
Push for HSCT efficacy message delivery improvement (HSCT4, HSCT5)
Propose cost/access message module or PSP awareness tracking

SharePoint: Project #26

Cross-Workstream Themes
1. Launch Tracking Demand Creates Natural Growth
Three simultaneous Merck launches — IDVYNSO (HIV, May 2026), KN-689 (HNSCC, ongoing post-June 2025), and Enflonsia (RSV, launched July 2025) — all require tracking research. ZoomRx is already positioned on two of three (KN-689 and Enflonsia) and has the SFE study locked for IDVYNSO. The launch wave is the most favorable BD environment for the account.
Implication for Vinoth: Ensure East 7 capacity can absorb the workload from these three concurrent launches. If a new win comes in (Gardasil PET reactivation), staffing becomes the constraint.
2. Keytruda Cross-Study Insights Are the Defensible Moat
The cross-study synthesis across 9 Keytruda studies represents ZoomRx's most defensible intellectual property on the account. No competitor can replicate the referral chain analysis, PD-L1 testing trajectory, MDT dynamics mapping, or community vs. academic adoption patterns without years of longitudinal access.
Implication for Vinoth: This cross-study capability should be surfaced to Rhonda Harp as evidence of ZoomRx's strategic value. It is the strongest argument for elevating ZoomRx from "tracking vendor" to "insights partner."
3. Under-Delivered High-Impact Messages Are a Common Pattern
Both Prevymis (KT7: "No CMV cases") and Enflonsia (ENF2: dosing simplicity — well-delivered but ENF10 under-recalled) show a pattern where the most effective messages are not the most frequently delivered. In HIV, Biktarvy's most effective messages are also under-recalled, but Gilead still leads on perceived effectiveness — structural positioning advantage.
Implication for Vinoth: Frame this as a cross-franchise insight in conversations with Merck: "We see a consistent pattern across your brands where your most compelling messages are under-delivered. This is trainable — and we can help you prioritize."
4. Franchise Diversification Is Essential for LOE Protection
Keytruda dominates the Merck account revenue. With LOE in 2028-2029, ZoomRx's Merck revenue is structurally exposed to Keytruda's franchise health. Diversifying into HIV (IDVYNSO), vaccines (Enflonsia, Gardasil), transplant (Prevymis), and pipeline (EyeBio, PCSK-9) is not just a growth strategy — it's a risk management imperative.
Implication for Vinoth: Track non-Keytruda revenue as a % of total Merck revenue. Target >40% non-Keytruda by FY28.

Immediate Priorities
